steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.Osculo: 'Osculo' is a noun in the ablative meaning 'with a kiss'
hints.Filium: 'Filium' is a noun meaning 'Son' in the accusative
hints.hominis: 'Hominis' is a noun meaning 'of man' in the genitive

hints.to give: 'To give' is 'dare' — 'tradere' means specifically 'to hand over/betray'

steps.reveal: tradis = you betray / you hand over (2nd sing. pres.)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'tradas' — 'tradis' is indicative

hints.traho, trahere, traxi, tractum: These are the parts of 'traho' (to drag/draw)
hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'duco' (to lead)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are the parts of 'do' (to give) — 'tradere' is the compound trans- + dare

steps.reveal: Osculo = with a kiss (abl. of means/instrument)

hints.dative: Dative 'osculo' looks the same, but the sense is 'by means of a kiss' — ablative of means

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'osculi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'osculi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'osculi' ends in -i

steps.reveal: The kiss is the instrument of betrayal

hints.ablative absolute: Ablative absolute requires noun + participle — 'Osculo' alone is not an ablative absolute
hints.ablative of manner: Ablative of manner describes how something is done (usually with 'cum') — this names the instrument
hints.ablative of place: Ablative of place would name a location — 'Osculo' names the means of betrayal

steps.reveal: Filium = the Son (acc., direct object of 'tradis')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Filius' — 'Filium' with -um is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'filii' ends in -ii
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'filii' ends in -ii
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'filii' ends in -ii

translation: Do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?
